title: abies pinsapo subsp. tazaotana
intro: Abies pinsapo subsp. tazaotana, also known as the Marokai fir, is a subspecies of the Atlas fir native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of compounds such as borneol and bornyl acetate. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Abies pinsapo subsp. tazaotana is often prepared as an essential oil or a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions or as a preventive measure.
